name: Shilajit
form: Purified resin · standardized for fulvic acid
shortDescription: Resin from Himalayan rocks, rich in 84+ trace minerals and fulvic acid.
longDescription: Shilajit seeps from cracks in Himalayan rocks at altitudes above 16,000 feet — formed over centuries from compressed plant matter. It carries 84+ trace minerals and fulvic acid, which acts as a transport molecule helping cells absorb nutrients. We standardize every batch for fulvic acid content (minimum 18%).

name: Ashwagandha
shortDescription: KSM-66 root extract — the most-studied form for cortisol regulation.
longDescription: Ashwagandha is an adaptogen — it doesn't push your body in one direction, it helps regulate. KSM-66 is the full-spectrum root extract; it has the strongest research backing for cortisol regulation and stress resilience. Best taken in the evening when your cortisol curve should be falling anyway.

name: Sea Moss
shortDescription: Red algae carrying iodine, magnesium, potassium, and 90+ trace minerals.
longDescription: Sea moss (Irish moss) is a red algae carrying iodine, magnesium, potassium, and 90+ trace minerals in bioavailable form. It's not a magic bullet — it's the floor that lets everything else work. If you've been mineral-deficient (most people are), you'll notice it within 2–3 weeks.

name: Turmeric
shortDescription: Curcumin paired with piperine for 20× bioavailability.
longDescription: Turmeric's active compound is curcumin. The catch: curcumin alone has very poor bioavailability — your body excretes most of it. We pair it with piperine (from black pepper) which boosts absorption 20×. Daily anti-inflammatory baseline.

name: Maca
shortDescription: Gelatinized Peruvian root — broken down for absorption.

name: Tongkat Ali
shortDescription: Standardized Eurycoma — 4–6 weeks to reach effect.
